metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4056
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3: Representative recordings of single-cell [Ca2+]i responses to KCl depolarization, in the presence of NPY2R agonist NPY13-36 .
Figure 4: Pre-synaptic modulation of [Ca2+]i by NPY receptors was tested on rat hippocampal synaptosomes. Fura-2 loaded synaptosomes were depolarized with KCl, resulting in a subsequent increase in [Ca2+]i. NPY2R agonist NPY13-36 inhibited the increase in [Ca2+]i upon KCl stimulation.
Figure 6a: The total glutamate release was found to be significantly decreased upon addition of NPY2R agonist. And the addition of the selective Y2 receptor antagonist, T4-[NPY(33-36)]4, completely prevented the inhibitory effect induced by NPY13-36, further solidifying the role of NPY2R in modulating glutamate release in hippocampal neurons.
Figure 7: The role of NPY2R in modulating glutamate release was specifically seen in all regions of the hippocampus: CA1, CA3 and DAG.

11/01/2021 Dnyanada: The paper demonstrates the involvement of neuropeptide receptor NPY2R in presynaptic inhibition of endogenous glutamate release. SynGO ID: 4036 (PMID: 29775703) shows that the NPY2R is a receptor specific for the neuropeptide NPY.. Experimental description: Hippocampal neurones were dissociated from hippocampi of E18-E19 Wistar rat embryos.
